item.title: Instance registry file written without mode 0600 — world-readable on disk
item.intent: The instance registry is written with mode 0600 — only the owning user can read it.
item.description: src/cloud/provisioning/registry.ts line 63 writes the instance registry file using default filesystem permissions. Unlike credentials.json which uses FILE_MODE_SECRET (0600), the registry uses no explicit mode. The registry contains provisioned instance data including IP addresses, provider IDs, and (after BUG-072) sshKeyPath. This data should be owner-read-only.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/registry.ts — find the file write call (around line 63)
Grep for FILE_MODE_SECRET in the codebase to find where 0600 is defined and how it is applied to credentials.json
Apply FILE_MODE_SECRET (or equivalent 0600) to the registry file write — match pattern used for credentials.json
Confirm no other registry read/write paths set a permissive mode
acceptanceCriteria: Instance registry file is written with mode 0600
Mode is applied using the same constant as credentials.json — no magic number
All four provider adapters tested confirm registry is written with 0600 after provisioning
